title: Toms Divorce

jokes.text: Come on. I don't want to go to the fourth floor. That is the creepiest place on earth.
jokes.setup: Being asked to go to the fourth floor for a mundane DMV task
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'the creepiest place on earth' with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Strong setup for the absurd description that follows, good character-specific cowardice

jokes.text: They put a popcorn machine up there just to brighten things up, but they used the wrong kind of oil and a bunch of people had to get their throats replaced.
jokes.setup: Describing why the fourth floor is creepy
jokes.punchline: Escalating from popcorn machine to throat replacement surgery
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation with perfect deadpan delivery. The specific detail of 'wrong kind of oil' makes it feel real while being completely ridiculous

jokes.text: They'll only talk to you or me, and I can't go. Because I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Setting up why Tom has to go instead of Leslie
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest admission that she just doesn't want to
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her fake authority crumbling into childish honesty. Economy is excellent

jokes.text: Hey, boo, you're pretty. Thank you, sir. Are you on probation? I got clean urine. You need female, I got female.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to navigate the sketchy fourth floor
jokes.punchline: Immediate offer of clean urine samples from a stranger
jokes.explanation: Good environmental comedy showing the seediness Tom was afraid of

jokes.text: Amazing. Took a risk, bought some shoes online. Paid off handsomely, as you can see.
jokes.setup: Being asked how everything in his life is going
jokes.punchline: Completely deflecting from his divorce to talk about shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his superficiality as defense mechanism. Strong misdirection from serious question

jokes.text: Honestly, it's fine. Lasted longer than Avril Lavigne and the guy from Sum 41. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Trying to downplay his divorce
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to an obscure celebrity marriage
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Tom's pop culture obsession and deflection tactics

jokes.text: I don't know what those are.
jokes.setup: Tom's reference to Avril Lavigne and Sum 41
jokes.punchline: Leslie's complete cultural disconnect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie response - her cultural obliviousness perfectly contrasts Tom's references

jokes.text: You should have auto-saved that. That kind of feels like your fault.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying it would have been nice to save Tom's marriage
jokes.punchline: Tom treating marriage advice like computer technical support
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor mixing marriage counseling with tech support. Perfectly Tom's millennial mindset

jokes.text: I did a little research, and divorce is the number two most stressful event in a person's life. Of course marriage is number seven. So watch out, everyone. It's all bad.
jokes.setup: Leslie doing research on divorce stress
jokes.punchline: Realizing marriage is also stressful, concluding 'it's all bad'
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her research leading to pessimistic conclusion

jokes.text: Tom always seems like Mr. Slickster cool guy, but he's hiding his emotions underneath a very thick layer of axe body spray.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Tom seems fine but isn't
jokes.punchline: The specific image of emotions hidden under Axe body spray
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor for Tom's character - the Axe body spray detail is specific and visual

jokes.text: Don't be all like, 'I don't want to. I am a guy, and I like fire and playing hockey and eating meat. No, no, says I.'
jokes.setup: Telling everyone not to be unsupportive
jokes.punchline: Her exaggerated impression of masculine stubbornness
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie impression of stereotypical masculinity, though slightly generic

jokes.text: That was a really good Ron. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Leslie doing her masculine impression
jokes.punchline: Ann recognizing it as specifically imitating Ron
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment acknowledging the impression's target

jokes.text: The shoeshine stand still doesn't have that syphilis medication you were asking about.
jokes.setup: Andy arriving at the office
jokes.punchline: Loudly announcing fake embarrassing medical needs for Mark
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Andy's sabotage campaign beginning

jokes.text: What a surprise running into you all day every day every single place that we are.
jokes.setup: Andy showing up at her workplace again
jokes.punchline: The mounting frustration building to 'every single place that we are'
jokes.explanation: Great escalating sarcasm with perfect rhythm building to the final phrase

jokes.text: we don't have those extra-small condoms you ordered. I called the factory. It's gonna take a special order. Not just because of the size, but because of the weird shape as well.
jokes.setup: Andy continuing his sabotage campaign
jokes.punchline: Escalating from small size to 'weird shape' requiring factory consultation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with the 'weird shape' detail pushing it into absurd territory

jokes.text: Every day I subtly chip away at their relationship, weakening it little by little.
jokes.setup: Andy's talking head after his sabotage attempts
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of subtlety while claiming to be subtle
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - audience knows his methods are anything but subtle

jokes.text: Leslie thinks you're a wounded animal, so her female instincts are kicking in. Here's what you do. Act sad. Let her pull the thorn out of your paw and wrap a bandage around it.
jokes.setup: Ron giving Tom advice about Leslie's mothering
jokes.punchline: The specific animal metaphor with thorn and bandage
jokes.explanation: Good Ron wisdom delivered through animal metaphor, very characteristic

jokes.text: Thanks, Rondoleezza Rice.
jokes.setup: Tom thanking Ron for advice
jokes.punchline: Combining Ron's name with Condoleezza Rice
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Tom's pop culture reference style

jokes.text: There's a really fun documentary about tandem bicycles at the art house. Supposed to be pretty unapologetic.
jokes.setup: Suggesting entertainment for Tom's sad night out
jokes.punchline: A documentary about tandem bicycles being described as 'unapologetic'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific Ann moment - using film critic language for something mundane. 'Unapologetic' is perfectly pretentious

jokes.text: Strip club? Did somebody say strip club? No. Somebody say strip club? You did. I definitely heard someone say strip club. You did.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions strip club
jokes.punchline: Jerry's eager repeated confirmation despite everyone's denials
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry character moment showing his inappropriate enthusiasm

jokes.text: There is a great dinosaur-themed restaurant in Patterson. It is called Jurassic Fork. I have gone there three times a week for the last 15 years.
jokes.setup: Ron suggesting a restaurant
jokes.punchline: The pun name 'Jurassic Fork' and his obsessive frequency of visits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - the pun is dad-joke level but his devotion makes it endearing

jokes.text: How do you want that cooked? Medium roar. Medium rare? No, medium roar.
jokes.setup: Server asking about meat temperature at dinosaur restaurant
jokes.punchline: Ron insisting on 'medium roar' instead of 'medium rare'
jokes.explanation: Great commitment to the bit by Ron, showing his thorough embrace of the theme

jokes.text: For legal reasons, we can't make puns about the temperature of the meats.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to order with dinosaur puns
jokes.punchline: The restaurant has legal restrictions on their own theming
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about themed restaurants having to protect themselves legally

jokes.text: I'm not a big fan of group dinners where everybody splits the bill no matter what they get. I ordered a tyranno-ceasar salad, and that's all I'm paying for.
jokes.setup: Jerry talking about splitting restaurant bills
jokes.punchline: His cheapness combined with using the dinosaur pun names
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry character moment showing his cheapness while still playing along with theme

jokes.text: No, I figured because pool is all about angles, and he's a failed architect, that he might want to play pool. Really? That worked?
jokes.setup: Andy seemingly making a clever psychological observation
jokes.punchline: His surprise that his random comment actually worked
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his accidental psychology working despite his lack of intent

jokes.text: I really wish I could have your body. What? Like, tied up, naked, in your basement?
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to compliment Tom's physique
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate assumption about serial killer implications
jokes.explanation: Great dark turn on an innocent compliment, creating maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: Well, that was weird, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Jerry's awkward body compliment
jokes.punchline: Tom's massive understatement calling it just 'weird'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to extremely uncomfortable moment

jokes.text: Tom is the only cool person in the office.
jokes.setup: Everyone giving compliments to Tom
jokes.punchline: April's backhanded compliment insulting everyone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect April delivery - complimenting Tom by insulting everyone else simultaneously

jokes.text: I love you, Tom. You're my little prince. Just want to put you in a little cape and a little hat and just fly you around.
jokes.setup: Donna giving Tom a compliment
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and infantilizing imagery
jokes.explanation: Great Donna moment showing her maternal instincts through absurd imagery

jokes.text: Can I get two creme brulees and another glass of 'bronto' grigio?
jokes.setup: Tom ordering more food and drink while pretending to be sad
jokes.punchline: Still committing to the dinosaur puns while milking sympathy
jokes.explanation: Good Tom manipulation combined with commitment to restaurant theming

jokes.text: I thought I had Tom all figured out, but it's almost like he's faking being sad. Why would he do that?
jokes.setup: Leslie in talking head about Tom's behavior
jokes.punchline: Her complete inability to imagine someone would fake sadness for attention
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie naivety - she can't fathom deception for personal gain

jokes.text: Step two has been completed. Easily. Very easily. Mark is pretty good at pool.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his pool hustling strategy
jokes.punchline: His overconfidence immediately deflating with admission Mark is good
jokes.explanation: Good Andy self-sabotage through overconfidence meeting reality

jokes.text: I like pretending to be sad. I now see why girls do it.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about his fake sadness
jokes.punchline: His casually sexist generalization about women
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's character flaws through casual sexism, though feels somewhat dated

jokes.text: Looking at her, I... I feel like... She might be the perfect spooning size for me.
jokes.setup: Ron talking about potentially dating Wendy
jokes.punchline: His awkward physical assessment delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - his practical approach to romance through physical logistics

jokes.text: Mark is way better than me. I'm gonna... I'm gonna say that there is at least... a chance... that I didn't think this through completely.
jokes.setup: Andy realizing his pool hustling plan is failing badly
jokes.punchline: His massive understatement about not thinking it through
jokes.explanation: Good Andy realization delivered with characteristic understatement

jokes.text: A t-shirt I tackled Eddie Vedder in. It's literally priceless.
jokes.setup: Andy offering collateral for the pool bet
jokes.punchline: The specific and absurd story behind the t-shirt's value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - combining celebrity encounter with physical violence as treasure

jokes.text: So if I win, I also get Ann. Okay, fine. If you win, then you get Ann.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to make the pool bet more fair
jokes.punchline: Mark casually agreeing to wager his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' problematic attitudes toward women

jokes.text: A cheese fountain? A ruby? A goose heart?
jokes.setup: Leslie offering Tom increasingly extravagant things
jokes.punchline: The progression from reasonable to bizarre with 'goose heart'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect Leslie over-the-top problem-solving

jokes.text: You're a club promoter? Aspiring.
jokes.setup: Tom mentioning he's a club promoter
jokes.punchline: Having to immediately downgrade to 'aspiring'
jokes.explanation: Good Tom deflation moment showing the gap between image and reality

jokes.text: Like blood from a pterodactyl after it's attacked by a T-Rex.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Tom's sadness pouring out
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre dinosaur metaphor connecting to Jurassic Fork
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback combining Leslie's dramatic nature with the dinosaur restaurant theme

jokes.text: I can't go back there. But if you see Jasmine, tell her she can keep Anthony, but I want my microwave back.
jokes.setup: Tom saying he can't go to the strip club
jokes.punchline: The specific domestic dispute details involving a microwave
jokes.explanation: Great Tom detail showing his priorities and past relationship drama

jokes.text: I've gone on record that if I had to have a stripper's name, it would be Equality.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her feminist credentials before going to strip club
jokes.punchline: Her hypothetical stripper name being 'Equality'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her earnest feminism creating the most unsexy stripper name possible

jokes.text: I kind of feel like Jane Goodall studying the chimps. 'Cause there are some feminist scholars who say that stripping is a feminist act.
jokes.setup: Ann at the strip club trying to maintain intellectual distance
jokes.punchline: Her academic rationalization of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good Ann character moment showing her need to intellectualize uncomfortable situations

jokes.text: There is a girl here that also works at Quiznos. She's really nice to me here but really mean to me at Quiznos.
jokes.setup: Jerry making observations at the strip club
jokes.punchline: The contrast in service quality between venues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry observation - finding mundane workplace dynamics in strip club

jokes.text: I'm gonna put these in places you've never heard of.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about putting dollar bills somewhere
jokes.punchline: His overconfident claim about exotic placement
jokes.explanation: Decent Tom bravado moment, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Smells like a wet mop in here. And I get the feeling that these women is running a low-grade fever.
jokes.setup: Ron assessing the strip club environment
jokes.punchline: His clinical observations about hygiene and health
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response - approaching strip club with practical health concerns

jokes.text: Strippers do nothing for me. I like a strong, salt of the earth, self-possessed woman at the top of her field, your Steffi Grafs, your Sheryl Swoopes.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his romantic preferences
jokes.punchline: His specific examples of accomplished female athletes
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron moment showing his respect for professional competence over sexuality

jokes.text: You're doing a bang-up job of looking sad about Wendy. Does she make scrambled eggs?
jokes.setup: Ron commenting on Tom's fake sadness performance
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifting to practical breakfast concerns
jokes.explanation: Great Ron moment - acknowledging Tom's performance then focusing on food priorities

jokes.text: Take it down a notch. You already won your Oscar, DiCaprio.
jokes.setup: Ron telling Tom to dial back his fake sadness
jokes.punchline: Comparing Tom's overacting to Leonardo DiCaprio
jokes.explanation: Good Ron pop culture reference with practical application to Tom's situation

jokes.text: I'm awesome at pool, and I hustled your ass.
jokes.setup: Andy winning the pool game through luck
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of what happened
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his ability to completely misread situations in his favor

jokes.text: I know that legally Ann is now mine, but, it weirdly doesn't feel that way.
jokes.setup: Andy winning Ann in the pool bet
jokes.punchline: His confusion about why legal ownership doesn't feel real
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his dim understanding of human relationships

jokes.text: This is Seabiscuit. Sierra. Sorry, it's loud in here.
jokes.setup: Leslie being introduced to a stripper
jokes.punchline: Her mishearing the name as a famous racehorse
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her awkwardness in unfamiliar environments

jokes.text: And then afterwards, reconsider your profession, but for now, grind away.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving instructions to the stripper
jokes.punchline: Her feminist concern mixed with practical task completion
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie contradiction - wanting to help while also accomplishing her mission

jokes.text: Never mind. Thank you, Seabiscuit. That'll be all.
jokes.setup: Leslie dismissing the stripper after Tom refuses the dance
jokes.punchline: Still calling her Seabiscuit like dismissing a servant
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the name confusion with Leslie's formal dismissal

jokes.text: I've been to the Glitter Factory a million times. That girl up there, she's my emergency contact.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about his strip club experience
jokes.punchline: The escalation to having a stripper as emergency contact
jokes.explanation: Great Tom character detail showing how his lifestyle has blurred normal boundaries

jokes.text: It's exactly how I felt when that planet earth series ended.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to relate to feeling devastated when something ends
jokes.punchline: Her comparison to a nature documentary series
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her earnest comparison of divorce to documentary ending

jokes.text: I wish I wasn't alive to hear myself say this, but I am ashamed to be your deputy.
jokes.setup: Leslie confronting Ron about asking out Wendy
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic escalation to wishing she wasn't alive
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie dramatic overreaction showing her moral outrage

jokes.text: From the knees. He weighs eight pounds.
jokes.setup: Asking Ron to carry drunk Tom
jokes.punchline: Her lifting advice and Tom's tiny weight
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment combining helpful advice with Tom's small stature

jokes.text: You insensitive little hussy.
jokes.setup: Leslie confronting Wendy about having men over
jokes.punchline: Her Victorian-era insult delivered with modern outrage
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment using old-fashioned moral language in modern context

jokes.text: You should get married and start a club for people who betray Tom Haverford.
jokes.setup: Leslie angry at Ron and Wendy
jokes.punchline: Her suggestion for a very specific betrayal-themed club
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie dramatic suggestion showing her loyalty and organization instincts

jokes.text: You know those hangover pills you can order on TV? I threw up a bunch of them this morning and feel much better.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining how he's feeling after his hangover
jokes.punchline: Vomiting the hangover cure as the actual cure
jokes.explanation: Good Tom logic - accidentally finding a solution through misunderstanding the product

jokes.text: My God, because you're Libyan. No, damn it. Wendy's from Canada.
jokes.setup: Tom revealing the green card marriage
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate assumption about Tom being foreign, then the mundane reality of Canada
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection from exotic foreign intrigue to boring Canadian immigration

jokes.text: You remembered how much I like to say 'psych'.
jokes.setup: Ann asking Andy not to say 'psych'
jokes.punchline: Andy's appreciation that she remembered his verbal tic
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his simple appreciation for being known

jokes.text: Goodbye, A-cakes.
jokes.setup: Andy saying goodbye to Ann
jokes.punchline: His sweet nickname combining her name with pancakes
jokes.explanation: Sweet Andy moment showing his genuine affection through silly nickname

jokes.text: You like your wife? That's a bummer. Anyone knows that you like your wife? Your wife knows that you like your wife?
jokes.setup: Learning that Tom actually likes Wendy
jokes.punchline: Andy's confusion escalating through increasingly basic questions
jokes.explanation: Great Andy moment showing his genuine confusion about normal relationship dynamics

jokes.text: My crotch looks like a disco ball.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about glitter still being on him
jokes.punchline: The specific visual comparison to a disco ball
jokes.explanation: Good Tom visual image combining strip club aftermath with fashion consciousness

tmdb_overview: After Leslie finds out that Tom is getting a divorce, the department takes him out on the town to cheer him up. Meanwhile, Andy tries to hustle Mark in a game of pool.
insight: Tom's marital collapse generates 66 jokes in 24 minutes—Parks' densest episode to date.
